title: Journey Inside the Atom Worksheet MCQs
subject: Science

questions.question: A student claims that if Thomson's atomic model were correct, almost all alpha particles would pass through a gold foil without undergoing large deflections. Which statement best justifies this claim?
options: Electrons are concentrated at the centre of the atom.
Positive charge is uniformly distributed throughout the atom.
questions.answer: Positive charge is uniformly distributed throughout the atom.

questions.question: During Rutherford's gold foil experiment, only a very small fraction of alpha particles rebounded after striking the foil. This observation led to the conclusion that
options: Electrons possess greater mass than protons.
Most of the atom is occupied by the nucleus.
The nucleus occupies only a tiny fraction of the atom's volume.
Electrons remain fixed inside the nucleus.
questions.answer: The nucleus occupies only a tiny fraction of the atom's volume.

questions.question: An element X has an atomic number of 17 and a mass number of 37. Which of the following correctly represents the number of neutrons present in its atom?
Which statement correctly explains why atoms are electrically neutral?
options: Number of neutrons equals number of protons.
Number of electrons equals number of neutrons.
Number of electrons equals number of protons.
Number of protons equals mass number.
questions.answer: Number of electrons equals number of protons.

questions.question: A neutral atom has the electronic configuration 2,8,2. The valency of this element is
Which pair of atoms represents isotopes?
options: Atomic numbers: 18 and 20; Mass numbers: 40 and 40
Atomic numbers: 17 and 17; Mass numbers: 35 and 37
Atomic numbers: 11 and 12; Mass numbers: 23 and 24
Atomic numbers: 6 and 8; Mass numbers: 12 and 16
questions.answer: Atomic numbers: 17 and 17; Mass numbers: 35 and 37

questions.question: An atom contains 15 protons, 16 neutrons and 15 electrons. Which notation correctly represents the atom?
Which scientist successfully explained why electrons do not continuously lose energy while revolving around the nucleus?
An atom has the electronic configuration 2,8,7. During chemical reactions, the atom is most likely to
Which observation from Rutherford's experiment directly disproved Thomson's atomic model?
options: Most alpha particles passed straight through the foil.
A few alpha particles were deflected through very large angles.
questions.answer: A few alpha particles were deflected through very large angles.

questions.question: An atom contains 20 neutrons and has an atomic number of 19. Its mass number is
Which statement correctly distinguishes atomic number from mass number?
options: Atomic number is always greater than mass number.
Atomic number equals the number of protons, whereas mass number equals the total number of protons and neutrons.
questions.answer: Atomic number equals the number of protons, whereas mass number equals the total number of protons and neutrons.

questions.question: Which electronic configuration represents an atom having zero valency?
A student concludes that electrons contribute significantly to the mass of an atom. Which statement best evaluates this conclusion?
options: Correct, because electrons are heavier than protons.
Incorrect, because electrons possess negligible mass compared to protons and neutrons.
Correct, because electrons determine the mass number.
Incorrect, because electrons are positively charged.
questions.answer: Incorrect, because electrons possess negligible mass compared to protons and neutrons.

questions.question: Which of the following observations best supports the idea that electrons are present in every atom?
options: Radioactivity is observed in all elements.
Cathode rays show identical properties irrespective of the gas and electrode material used.
Every atom contains the same number of neutrons.
questions.answer: Cathode rays show identical properties irrespective of the gas and electrode material used.

questions.question: Which pair correctly represents isobars?
A neutral atom has 18 electrons. The number of protons present in its nucleus will be
Which statement correctly explains why isotopes of an element exhibit nearly identical chemical properties?
options: They contain the same number of neutrons.

questions.question: An atom has the electronic configuration 2,8,3. Which of the following is most likely to occur during compound formation?
A teacher asks students why the discovery of the neutron was necessary even after protons had been discovered. The most appropriate explanation is that neutrons
options: Explain the additional mass present in atomic nuclei.
Determine the atomic number of an element.
Revolve around the nucleus in fixed shells.
questions.answer: Explain the additional mass present in atomic nuclei.
